tweets.title:
BREAKING: Chinese President Xi Jinping has arrived in North Korea for his first visit to Pyongyang since 2019.

Xi was welcomed by Kim Jong Un amid growing China–North Korea coordination. The visit comes as Beijing seeks to reassert influence over its key ally, which has grown closer to Russia in recent years.

Denuclearization, regional security, and strategic cooperation are expected to top the agenda.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Chinese President Xi Jinping has arrived in North Korea for his first visit to Pyongyang since 2019.

Xi was welcomed by Kim Jong Un amid growing China–North Korea coordination. The visit comes as Beijing seeks to reassert influence over its key ally, which has grown closer to Russia in recent years.

Denuclearization, regional security, and strategic cooperation are expected to top the agenda.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
7th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

●  The IDF eliminated Sakr Abu Karim, a Hamas Nukhba cell commander.

● The IDF issued an urgent evacuation warning for residents of Tyre and nearby camps, citing repeated Hezbollah ceasefire violations.

● The US shot down two Iranian drones after they allegedly threatened international maritime traffic.

● Qatar issued a NOTAM rerouting flights through its airspace, providing alternate routes for aircraft departing Doha and Saudi airports amid ongoing regional tensions.

● The IRGC Ground Force’s Quds HQ, in a joint operation with intelligence forces, dismantled a terrorist cell attempting to infiltrate the country in the Saravan area.

● OPEC+ is set to approve a fourth consecutive monthly increase in oil output targets, despite the ongoing US-Iran war disrupting production for several member countries.

● As of June 7, CENTCOM has redirected 132 commercial vessels and disabled 6 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
7th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated Sakr Abu Karim, a Hamas Nukhba cell commander.

● The IDF issued an urgent evacuation warning for residents of Tyre and nearby camps, citing repeated Hezbollah ceasefire violations.

● The US shot down two Iranian drones after they allegedly threatened international maritime traffic.

● Qatar issued a NOTAM rerouting flights through its airspace, providing alternate routes for aircraft departing Doha and Saudi airports amid ongoing regional tensions.

● The IRGC Ground Force’s Quds HQ, in a joint operation with intelligence forces, dismantled a terrorist cell attempting to infiltrate the country in the Saravan area.

● OPEC+ is set to approve a fourth consecutive monthly increase in oil output targets, despite the ongoing US-Iran war disrupting production for several member countries.

● As of June 7, CENTCOM has redirected 132 commercial vessels and disabled 6 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
6th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran launched missile strikes targeting Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait and key facilities of the US Navy’s Fifth Fleet in Bahrain.

● CENTCOM forces shot down 4 Iranian one-way attack drones heading toward the Strait of Hormuz. US forces subsequently struck Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in Goruk and on Qeshm Island.

● Bahrain’s air defences intercepted and destroyed 3 missiles and several drones launched from Iran.

● Kuwait’s armed forces detected and intercepted 7 hostile ballistic missiles in its airspace.

tweets.title:
RT by @Goreunit: Brutal crackdown by Pakistan in PoK ahead of the June 9 shutdown.

Pakistani Army, Rangers and Punjab Police have opened fire on Awami Action Committee protesters, leaving 4 people seriously injured.

Internet services remain suspended, AAC offices have been sealed, and the group has been designated a terrorist organization.

Just a day earlier, AAC member Umar Nazir was seriously injured after Pakistani security forces allegedly opened fire on his vehicle, killing one of his associates.

The developments have revived memories of last year’s deadly crackdown in which 28 protesters were killed.

PoK is on edge.

tweets.title:
As a kid I read a chacha Chaudhry story 
It said :
A thief was caught and given a choice:
Either eat 100 onions or receive 100 shoe-beatings (sau jute).

Thinking onions are easier, he starts eating them. After a few onions, the burning becomes unbearable, so he switches to shoe-beatings.

After a few shoe-beatings, he finds those even worse and switches back to onions.

He keeps changing his mind until, in the end, he has eaten all 100 onions and received all 100 shoe-beatings.

Moral : Failure to commit to a difficult choice makes things worse.
tweets.content:
As a kid I read a chacha Chaudhry story
It said :
A thief was caught and given a choice:
Either eat 100 onions or receive 100 shoe-beatings (sau jute).

Thinking onions are easier, he starts eating them. After a few onions, the burning becomes unbearable, so he switches to shoe-beatings.

After a few shoe-beatings, he finds those even worse and switches back to onions.

He keeps changing his mind until, in the end, he has eaten all 100 onions and received all 100 shoe-beatings.

Moral : Failure to commit to a difficult choice makes things worse.

BREAKING: China Launches ‘Special Operation’ East of Taiwan

China has conducted a “special maritime law enforcement operation” in waters east of Taiwan, according to state media.

tweets.title:
Over the last few days, I’ve met founders of several defence startups. One theme came up repeatedly: “Failure is a step in the right direction. The more we fail, the better we get.”

Yet many of India’s major defence programmes have operated in an ecosystem where the appetite for failure is extremely low. Kaveri is perhaps the most cited example.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
6th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran launched missile strikes targeting Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait and key facilities of the US Navy’s Fifth Fleet in Bahrain.

● CENTCOM forces shot down 4 Iranian one-way attack drones heading toward the Strait of Hormuz. US forces subsequently struck Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in Goruk and on Qeshm Island.

● Bahrain’s air defences intercepted and destroyed 3 missiles and several drones launched from Iran.

● Kuwait’s armed forces detected and intercepted 7 hostile ballistic missiles in its airspace.

● The US State Department approved a military equipment sale to Kuwait that includes counter-unmanned aerial vehicle (C-UAV) systems.

● The Chinese-owned oil tanker JV INNOVATION, bulk carrier JIN YUAN MEN, and Singapore-registered oil tanker ALORA successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
5th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated senior members of Hamas’ General Security Apparatus, as well as Abed Harb, commander of Hezbollah’s Engineering Unit.

● The Israeli Air Force conducted airstrikes on multiple towns in southern Lebanon, including a strike on the town of Kafra in the Bint Jbeil district.

● US and Israel have begun formal negotiations on a new security framework to replace the current MOU expiring in 2028, shifting toward a reciprocal defense partnership with deeper joint military development.

● A drone struck the oil export terminal at Oman’s Mina Al-Fahal port, causing an explosion. Omani authorities stated operations continue as normal.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 129 commercial vessels and disabled 6 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title:
State-owned Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd is emerging as the lead anchor investor for a massive ₹29,253 crore mega shipbuilding cluster in Dugarajapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.

The ambitious maritime manufacturing hub will span approximately 3,500 acres and target 1.2 million gross tonnage of annual shipbuilding capacity.
